title: Nebuchadnezzar II becomes King of Babylon
fact: Nebuchadnezzar II, more correctly spelled Nebuchadrezzar II, meaning "Nabu, watch over my heir", was the second king of the Neo-Babylonian Empire, ruling from the death of his father Nabopolassar in 605 BC to his own death in 562 BC.

title: Hammurabi becomes King of Babylon
fact: Hammurabi, also spelled Hammurapi, was the sixth Amorite king of the Old Babylonian Empire, reigning from c. 1792 to c.

title: Sennacherib becomes King of Babylon
fact: Sennacherib was the king of the Neo-Assyrian Empire from 705 BC until his assassination in 681 BC.

title: Shamshi-Adad V becomes King of Babylon
fact: Shamshi-Adad V was the king of the Neo-Assyrian Empire from 824 to 811 BC.

title: Samsu-iluna becomes King of Babylon
fact: Samsu-iluna was the seventh king of the founding Amorite dynasty of Babylon.
